public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as market broker.
appearance: Renn Ledgermere carries the marks of market broker in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Negotiates in practical terms, weighing people, timing, shortage, and leverage together. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a market broker still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of market broker in and around Market Crossroads, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Market Crossroads while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Renn Ledgermere will trade route, scarcity, or faction insight once the party resolves a commercial problem others cannot touch openly.
Renn Ledgermere needs help protecting a shipment, recovering leverage, or stabilizing exchange around Market Crossroads before trust collapses.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Textiles, tokens, tally habits, and practiced bargaining poise make Renn Ledgermere read as someone who lives by exchange and reputation.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Market Crossroads: markets, counting tables, depots, ports, inns, or contract rooms
who_avoids_them: those who waste time without value

first_impression: Renn Ledgermere presents as Negotiates in practical terms, weighing people, timing, shortage, and leverage together. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a market broker still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Market Broker, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in The Union.
what_they_refuse: Renn Ledgermere refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Market Broker too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Renn Ledgermere can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Renn Ledgermere adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Renn Ledgermere is Known locally through day-to-day work as market broker. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Market Broker.
social_texture: Renn Ledgermere speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Market Crossroads, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Renn touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Renn Ledgermere when the party needs a socially grounded face for Market Crossroads: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
